steps.title: Notice the category problem
steps.content: The passage says thalassocracies are sea powers and tellurocracies are land-based powers. The British Empire complicates that distinction because it extended its power across both land and sea.

steps.title: Signal a blurred distinction
steps.content: Choice A, Choice B, and Choice C all suggest that the British Empire supports or illustrates the sea-power versus land-power distinction. It does the opposite: it makes the line less clear by spanning both categories. The answer is D.
